title: FTP service exposed
description: An FTP service is exposed on the standard port. Publicly reachable FTP increases the likelihood of credential attacks and insecure data transfer.

title: SSH service exposed
description: An SSH service is externally exposed. Confirm remote administration is required and locked down to trusted source ranges.

title: HTTP service exposed on port 80
description: A web service is exposed over HTTP on port 80. Evaluate whether TLS redirection and access restrictions are required.

title: SMB exposed
description: SMB is exposed on port 445. Internet-facing SMB significantly increases risk of unauthorized file access and lateral movement.

title: MSSQL exposed
description: Microsoft SQL Server is reachable on the default port. Database services should not be exposed to untrusted networks.

title: PPTP exposed
description: A PPTP endpoint is exposed. PPTP is a legacy VPN technology and should be replaced or tightly controlled.

title: MySQL exposed
description: MySQL is externally reachable on port 3306. Restrict direct database access to trusted internal networks.

title: SIP exposed
description: SIP signaling is exposed on port 5060. Public exposure can lead to toll fraud attempts and service abuse.

title: alternate HTTP service exposed
description: An HTTP-like service is exposed on a non-standard port. Verify whether this endpoint is intended for public access.

title: HTTPS-alt exposed
description: An HTTPS or management interface is reachable on port 8443. Administrative interfaces should be isolated from public networks.

title: Port 9200 exposed
description: Elasticsearch API port 9200 is publicly exposed. This can enable data exfiltration and unauthorized index access.

title: Administrative web interface exposed on uncommon port
description: A likely administrative or management web interface is exposed on a non-standard port based on service banner evidence.

title: HTTP service exposed on alternate web ports
description: HTTP service exposure on alternate ports increases attack surface and often indicates admin endpoints.

title: HTTPS service exposed on 443
description: HTTPS endpoint is externally reachable. Confirm this exposure is intended and protected by hardened TLS and auth controls.

title: Apache HTTP Server exposed
description: Apache HTTP Server is externally exposed. Validate if public exposure is required and hardened.
